title: COPY command is not watching for changes in time stamp on SOURCE dir/files
body:
Simple Containerfile

```
$ cat /tmp/Containerfile 
FROM scratch
COPY foobar /foobar
ENTRYPOINT []
$ echo bar >>/tmp/foobar
```

Build
```
$ buildah build --layers /tmp
STEP 1/3: FROM scratch
STEP 2/3: COPY foobar /foobar
--> 59cc80876e6b
STEP 3/3: ENTRYPOINT []
COMMIT
--> b94b28246eac
b94b28246eac28f100098916f0b914a36e54d0f67106210c8d864fa76457eac7
```

Modify source file:
```
echo foobar > /tmp/foobar
```

Rebuild 
```
$ buildah build --layers /tmp
STEP 1/3: FROM scratch
STEP 2/3: COPY foobar /foobar
--> Using cache c48f224de5c2b614927235ec8f7a8e89d4f7518e3fc986c94a5bc9f931deb99d
--> c48f224de5c2
STEP 3/3: ENTRYPOINT []
--> Using cache d84b0edf3ef5708da43dcacc729088bc484873489fa1654aa5f86df08e091745
--> d84b0edf3ef5
d84b0edf3ef5708da43dcacc729088bc484873489fa1654aa5f86df08e091745
```

The second build should have realized that the foobar file had changed and not used the cache.

comments.body: I had to go look, but ~this~ https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/2546 made us start ignoring timestamps so that we would get a cache hit for content that was checked out from git, which doesn't preserve timestamps on files.  Changing permissions or ownership with `--chmod` or `--chown`, or changing the file contents, should force a cache miss.  Is the current behavior creating problems?
I am modifying the content of /tmp/foobar above and it still reports its using the cache.

$ cp /etc/passwd /tmp/foobar 
$ ls -l /tmp/foobar 
-rw-r--r--. 1 dwalsh dwalsh 3437 Apr  2 18:08 /tmp/foobar
ramalama (images) $ buildah build --layers /tmp
STEP 1/3: FROM scratch
STEP 2/3: COPY foobar /foobar
--> ddd9b5a4d1e4
STEP 3/3: ENTRYPOINT []
COMMIT
--> d0e51e7d7c63
d0e51e7d7c638127eaccaac47ca17227ab9f77f0a0c9a2d870add0f7c9bfb8db
$ echo hi > /tmp/foobar
$ ls -l /tmp/foobar 
-rw-r--r--. 1 dwalsh dwalsh 3 Apr  2 18:08 /tmp/foobar
$ buildah build --layers /tmp
STEP 1/3: FROM scratch
STEP 2/3: COPY foobar /foobar
--> Using cache c48f224de5c2b614927235ec8f7a8e89d4f7518e3fc986c94a5bc9f931deb99d
--> c48f224de5c2
STEP 3/3: ENTRYPOINT []
--> Using cache d84b0edf3ef5708da43dcacc729088bc484873489fa1654aa5f86df08e091745
--> d84b0edf3ef5
d84b0edf3ef5708da43dcacc729088bc484873489fa1654aa5f86df08e091745
Ok I think I am being fooled by caching.
